[[File:Deathlok-Rockets-S1E22.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok aims at [[Phil Coulson]] and [[Nick Fury]]]] Ever confident that he could never be defeated and now able to withstand normal gunshots to the chest, Garrett mocked their efforts, claiming that this was a strange fight between three dead men, considering they had all [[Resurrection of Phil Coulson|seemingly returned]] from [[Attack on Nick Fury|the dead]] recently. The three continued throwing insults at each other while Deathlok stood silently by, with Garrett misquoting one of Fury's speeches and claiming to be the origin of all things before ordering Deathlok to take aim at Coulson and Fury with his [[Forearm Rocket Launcher]], which he did, awaiting Garrett's order final to fire upon them both. [[File:Deathlok-attacks-Garrett.png|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok stomps on [[John Garrett]]'s head]] Unknown to Garrett, however, [[Skye]] had become Peterson's newest handler throughout this exchange, having successfully captured [[Kyle Zeller]] and overriding all the computer systems to get into Deathlok's programming. Ultimately, Skye had found and freed [[Ace Peterson|Ace]] and assured Peterson that the [[Backscatter X-Ray Eye Implant]] would not be used against him and that his son was safe. Deathlok then responded by furiously shooting Garrett with his [[Forearm Rocket Launcher]]. Despite Garrett's desperate pleas for him to stop, Peterson crushed his head with his [[Cybertek Prosthetic Leg|Prosthetic Leg]], seemingly killing Garrett. [[File:Deathlok-leaves.png|{{AltFloat}}|Peterson leaves to try and find redemption]] As the last of [[Hydra]] operatives were arrested and Ace was reunited with his aunt [[Mindy Peterson|Mindy]], Peterson watched from afar with Skye, too ashamed to approach and fearful of what his son's reaction to him would be. When Skye had asked him to join [[Coulson's Team]], he declined, deciding that he wanted redemption before developing a relationship with anyone. He told her that they were welcome to hack into his [[Backscatter X-Ray Eye Implant|Cybernetic Implants]] and track his every move, because all he would be doing would be making amends for his past actions. With this he left to begin his mission to protect innocent lives.{{Ref|AoS122}} ===Second S.H.I.E.L.D.=== ====Hunting Down Doctor List==== [[File:Deathlok Is Back.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok comes to [[Phil Coulson]]'s rescue]] {{Quote|I intercepted a coded transmission from [[List]], mentioning his interest in [[Inhumans|powered people]]."
"Yeah, they seem to be trending these days."
"He's hunting them down and experimenting on them.|Deathlok and [[Lance Hunter]]|Afterlife (episode)}} Continuing his mission to use all his powers for good, Peterson was given upgrades to his arsenal by [[Jemma Simmons]], including his new EMP Launcher.{{Ref|AoS217}} Peterson accepted [[Phil Coulson]]'s invitation to join the newly rebuilt [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] as an agent with Coulson as its new director. Coulson then sent Peterson overseas to dismantle [[Hydra]] powers there and pursue [[List]], which Peterson did for six months. During this time, he discovered List's intent of hunting down newly discovered [[Inhumans|powered people]] and then horrifically experimenting on them. [[File:Deathlok-shoots-Quinjet-Afterlife.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok manages to shoot down a [[Quinjet]]]] However, Coulson's attempts to rebuild S.H.I.E.L.D. had caused him to come into conflict with [[Robert Gonzales]] who had also started an alternative branch and was currently attempting to have Coulson arrested. While attempting to capture a [[Quinjet]] to escape Gonzales' agents, Coulson called Peterson to meet him at the [[Retreat]]. While he was waiting, Coulson then alerted Gonzales' faction of S.H.I.E.L.D. to his current presence there, and they responded with several teams of agents. However, Peterson was unable to arrive before Coulson and [[Lance Hunter]] were soon captured by Gonzales' agents. [[File:DeathlokMeetsLanceHunter.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok introduces himself to [[Lance Hunter]]]] Before they could be taken to the [[Playground]], Peterson intercepted them, using his incredible strength from the [[Centipede Serum]] to pull an entire Quinjet back down onto the ground before charging in and confronting Gonzales' agents. Once he had knocked out the agents guarding Coulson, he stepped outside and used his [[Forearm Rocket Launcher]] to fire an EMP into the second Quinjet, causing it to crash land without killing the men inside. Deathlok then freed Coulson and Hunter, introducing himself to Hunter who was shocked by his power and pained when he crushed his hand during their handshake. [[File:Deathlok-discusses-List.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok discusses his hunt for [[List]]]] Once they were all safe, Deathlok hacked into the Quinjet's controls and piloted them away from the danger while Hunter and Coulson discussed the thrill of having him come to their rescue, with Hunter surprised to have been saved by a cyborg. Once they were safe and Peterson put the Quinjet into autopilot, Peterson then explained that during his hunt for List he had discovered that Hydra was experimenting on powered people. Believing List's knowledge could help them find a [[Gordon|teleporter]] who had taken [[Skye]], they agreed to find and recruit [[Grant Ward]].{{Ref|AoS216}} ====Building the Team==== [[File:Deathlok-greets-Leo-Fitz-Quinjet.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok successfully rescuing [[Leo Fitz]]]] {{Quote|Fact that this guy hasn't killed me yet means they need me. Mike."
"Ward, turn around. You know what needs to happen.|[[Grant Ward]] and Deathlok|The Frenemy of My Enemy}} Alongside [[Phil Coulson]] and [[Lance Hunter]], Peterson set up a plan to rescue [[Leo Fitz]] in [[San Francisco]] with their [[Quinjet]]. When Fitz knew he being followed by [[Robert Gonzales]]' soldiers, Fitz ran as fast as possible onto a roof where Peterson was waiting in the Quinjet. The Quinjet was cloaked so it appeared invisible to the enemy soldiers who proceeded to run straight into it. As they were taking off, Peterson greeted his old friend who asked about Peterson's upgrades before Coulson informed Fitz of their plan to recruit [[Grant Ward]] to help them. [[File:DeathlokMeetsAgent33.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok watches over the captured [[Kara Palamas]]]] The team then immediately traveled toward [[Tijuana]] in order to recruit Ward as well as [[Kara Palamas]]. Peterson went in first and held Palamas hostage, Coulson then took her phone and ordered Ward to come for a meeting, explaining that he was holding Palamas at gunpoint until he arrived. While they waited, Peterson had asked Palamas about the [[Photostatic Veil]] she wore to hide her facial scars, asking if she had any more which he could use. Palamas confessed that she did not have any other Veils, to which Deathlok joked that at least he still had his winning personality which could mask his own burn scars. [[File:13-2 595 Mini-Logo-TV-white---Gallery.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok tells [[Grant Ward]] to drop his weapon]] When Ward arrived, Peterson greeted him and using his x-ray vision from his [[Backscatter X-Ray Eye Implant]], located all the weapons Ward was carrying and ordered him to set down the knives and guns, which Ward was left with no choice but to obey. Ward then had a meeting with [[Phil Coulson]] while Deathlok kept watch to ensure that Ward did not attempt to kill Coulson. During the meeting Coulson offered to erase Ward's memories with [[Project T.A.H.I.T.I.]] to help him build a new life if he helped them on this mission to infiltrate [[Hydra]] and locate [[Skye]]. Confessing that he knew of how to get access onto [[Baron Strucker]], Ward agreed to help Coulson.{{Ref|AoS218}} ====Working with Grant Ward==== [[File:Deathlok-NightChat-S2E18.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok and [[Phil Coulson]] discuss the plan]] {{Quote|Get the [[Sunil Bakshi|package]]?"
"Just like they said. I still don't trust them."
"Hell, no, but we need them to trust us.|[[Phil Coulson]] and Deathlok|The Frenemy of My Enemy}} Having agreed to assist in their mission, [[Grant Ward]] then revealed that he and [[Kara Palamas]] had successfully [[Kidnapping of Sunil Bakshi|kidnapped]] and brainwashed [[Sunil Bakshi]] by using the [[Faustus]] method, all while [[Hydra]] had still believed Bakshi had been locked away by the [[United States Armed Forces]]. Deathlok then transported Ward and Palamas to Bakshi's location and locked him in the boot of the car before driving back to meet with [[Phil Coulson]], confirming to him that they did have the brainwashed Bakshi. [[File:16-2 595 Mini-Logo-TV-white---Gallery.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok looks at the captured [[Sunil Bakshi]]]] While Deathlok spoke with Coulson about the plan, he noted that he still did not trust Ward and Palamas following all their conflict with Ward while he had worked with [[John Garrett]]; however, Coulson noted that while he also did not trust Ward, he had needed him to trust them for their mission to be successful. Meanwhile, Ward and Palamas were sat in the car together, discussing if they could trust Coulson and his new [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] while they also expressed their love for each other until Deathlok interrupted them and ordered them out of the car. They all then stood around the car boot so they could give all of their new orders to Bakshi with Coulson looking at the nervous Bakshi. [[File:Deathlok-Flies-Quinjet-S2E18.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok ordering [[Grant Ward]] to be quiet]] A plan was worked out that they would use Bakshi's high-ranking status to gain access to the current leaders of Hydra, including [[Baron Strucker]] who had been performing barbaric experiments on enhanced people to discover how their power could be replicated. Peterson was able to pose as Bakshi's bodyguard to attend a meeting between Bakshi and [[List]]. They claimed that Deathlok was responsible for breaking Bakshi out of prison, reminding List of all the work [[John Garrett]] had put into creating him during [[Project Deathlok]]. [[File:Deathlok-RedEye-S2E18.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok watches over [[Sunil Bakshi]] and [[List]]]] During List and Bakshi's conversation, Peterson quietly listened while his eye-piece allowed Coulson and the team to watch closely. Eventually Bakshi won over List's trust, assuring him he was not to blame for the [[Assassination of Hydra Leaders]] and revealed that he intended to trade Deathlok to List to be used for his experiments, in exchange for a meeting with Strucker. Mortified at the thought of being tortured to death, Peterson readied his weapons for a fight for freedom, but was ordered by Coulson to play along, which he reluctantly obeyed although remaining on edge.{{Ref|AoS218}} ====[[Battle at Calvin Zabo's Building]]==== [[File:4-2 595 Mini-Logo-TV-white---Gallery.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok searches the building for [[Gordon]]]] {{Quote|Whoever you are, you don't want to do this. I'm a friend of [[Skye]]'s."
"Sure. And I'm the [[Hulk]].|Deathlok and [[Lincoln Campbell]]|The Frenemy of My Enemy}} [[List]] was informed of the location of a group of powered individuals, and before they went to meet [[Baron Strucker]], List sent [[Sunil Bakshi]], Deathlok and a team of [[Hydra]] operatives to capture the individuals as they had discovered the signal of a [[Gordon|teleporter]] they had been tracking. While the soldiers fought [[Calvin Zabo]] and [[Skye]], [[Phil Coulson]], [[Lance Hunter]], [[Kara Palamas]] and [[Grant Ward]] attacked the base at the same time, avoiding direct conflict with Deathlok while Hydra was present in order to still maintain his undercover mission with them. [[File:Lincoln-vs-Deathlok.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok is attacked by [[Lincoln Campbell]]]] While still walking through the hallways searching for the teleporter as though he was working for Hydra, Peterson encountered [[Lincoln Campbell]], who used electricity powers to shock Peterson, using the power to levitate Peterson. However, Campbell discovered that due to Deathlok's cybernetic enhancements the electric shock did not come close to stopping him, leading to Campbell questioning what he was made of to have survived the attack. As Deathlok tried to insist he was a friend of Skye's, Campbell did not believe him and claimed to be the [[Hulk]] as a joke before attacking him once again, engaging in a brief fight before reaching physical exhaustion. [[File:Deathlok-BetrayedByBakshi.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok getting betrayed by [[Sunil Bakshi]]]] While they lay injured together, Coulson and Ward came to their aid but Peterson ordered them to go after Skye, claiming that Campbell was an ally and not to be harmed. The electric attack did, however, damage Peterson's [[Backscatter X-Ray Eye Implant]] which [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] was still attempting to hack into. While Peterson and Campbell still lay on the ground, Bakshi then appeared and betrayed Peterson, and he and Campbell were then knocked out by a [[Pulse Grenade]] and captured by Hydra, ready to be taken to the [[Arctic Hydra Research Facility]] for List's experimentation on their enhancements.{{Ref|AoS218}} ====Captured by Hydra==== [[File:Deathlok-PrisonCell.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok being held captive by [[Hydra]]]] {{Quote|It's not as bad as it looks."
"Really?"
"Nah, it's pretty bad.|Deathlok and [[Jemma Simmons]]|The Dirty Half Dozen}} Peterson awoke in a cell within the [[Arctic Hydra Research Facility]] only to discover that all his weapons had been deactivated. When [[Lincoln Campbell]] awoke in the cell next to him, Peterson explained the situation to him. Campbell asked if Peterson could use his weapons to help them escape but Peterson assured him that they had been deactivated. Peterson asked if Campbell's [[Gordon|teleporter friend]] could help them escape, but Campbell told him it would be far too dangerous and could endanger his [[Inhumans|people]]. Seeing that they were helpless, Campbell introduced himself. Shortly afterward the pair were gassed and knocked out, ready for experimentation. [[File:Deathlok-PostTorture.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok after being tortured by Doctor [[List]]]] Both Peterson and Campbell were then taken away by [[List]], Peterson was forced to endure terrible torture during [[Hydra]]'s experiments, beginning by removing his [[Backscatter X-Ray Eye Implant]] and dismantling his [[Cybertek Prosthetic Leg|prosthetic leg]]. After hours of experiments, Peterson was returned to his cell, only to be found by [[Phil Coulson]]'s team including [[Skye]] and [[Jemma Simmons]]. Peterson sent Skye to save [[Lincoln Campbell]] while Simmons and [[Grant Ward]] tended to his injuries. When Simmons sent Ward away to find a stretcher to get Deathlok out, Deathlok asked her why she was working with Ward again and she explained she was taking an opportunity. [[File:Deathlok-EyePatch-Playground.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok is taken back to the [[Playground]]]] Deathlok was taken back to the [[Playground]] where he, [[Leo Fitz]] and Simmons discussed repairing his destroyed cybernetic parts so he could soon return to active field work for [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] Fitz explained that not only had his [[Cybertek Prosthetic Leg]] been removed, but the Hydra teams had dismantled it. Fitz assured him that they were sending him to a facility where he could be helped. When Deathlok asked how he had been found Fitz explained that [[Sunil Bakshi]] had led them there under Ward's orders. When Deathlok asked where Bakshi was, Simmons noted that he had been killed during the firefight.{{Ref|AoS219}} ====Return and Aiding Coulson==== [[File:Deathlok SHIELD Reinforcement.png|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok arriving inside of the [[Lighthouse]]]] {{Quote|What are you doing, Coulson? Pick up the [[Gravity Containment Device|doodad]]. Let's go!|Deathlok to [[Phil Coulson]]|The Real Deal}} Following his kidnapping by [[Hydra]] which resulted in losing several key parts of his body, Deathlok was saved and then rebuilt as [[Leo Fitz]] and [[Jemma Simmons]] promised him.{{Ref|AoS219}} Having heard that the [[Playground]] had been attacked by [[Aida]], killing several agents,{{Ref|AoS421}} Deathlok went to investigate and found [[James Davis]] wounded, so Deathlok took him for aid. Having saved other agents, Deathlok was contacted by [[Deke Shaw]] who was desperately searching for agents loyal to [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]], bringing Deathlok and the other agents to the [[Lighthouse]] to regroup with the other remaining members of their organization. [[File:DeathlokShootsHisDouble.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok shoots and destroys his [[Mike Peterson/Monolith Recreation|double]]]] Having arrived on board the [[Quinjet]], Deathlok and the other agents were greeted by [[Melinda May]] and [[Daisy Johnson]] before he then immediately came to [[Phil Coulson]]'s aid as he was attempting to stop an unstable portal to the [[Fear Dimension]] created by the destruction of multiple [[Monolith]]s. Deathlok found Coulson just as he was being choked by [[Mike Peterson/Monolith Recreation|dimensional manifestation]] of his former self, as Deathlok immediately shot the manifestation of himself in the head with his [[Forearm Rocket Launcher]] before ordering Coulson to get back onto his feet and then continue their mission. [[File:Deathlok TRD.png|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok fighting against all the recreations]] Before Deathlok and Coulson could reunite, they were attacked by the manifestation of [[Lash/Monolith Recreation|Lash]], with Deathlok pushing Coulson out of the way before leaping over Lash to avoid his attack and firing a rocket into his chest in mid-air, killing the manifestation. They then found themselves surrounded by the manifestations of the [[Vrellnexians]], as Deathlok and Coulson then joined forces to destroy all the terrifying creatures. With the threat gone, Deathlok watched as Coulson used the [[Gravity Containment Device]] to try and destroy the gateway to the Fear Dimension before it could do harm to the outside world. [[File:DeathlokCoulsonTeamUp-S5E12.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok watches [[Hive/Monolith Recreation|Hive]] coming to attack]] As the device activated into the gateway, it had then caused a gravity surge which almost sucked the pair straight inside the portal, with Deathlok grabbing ahold of Coulson and using his enhanced strength and [[Cybertek Prosthetic Leg]] to keep them away from the portal with all of his might. However, while they were still keeping away from the gateway, they were attacked by the manifestation of [[Hive/Monolith Recreation|Hive]] who then attempted to kill them both. Deathlok then responded by swinging Coulson around so he could hit Hive with his [[Phil Coulson's Prosthetic Hand|Prosthetic Hand]] before Deathlok then fired another rocket directly into Hive's face. [[File:DeathlokCoulson-AwkwardSituation.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok and [[Phil Coulson]] sit awkwardly]] With all of the manifestations destroyed, the portal was finally closed and was contained in the [[Gravity Containment Device]], with Deathlok and Coulson falling back as gravity returned to normal. Finding Coulson had fallen onto his lap, Deathlok was questioned about whether or not he was real or a creature from the [[Fear Dimension]], with Deathlok questioning if him being real would make the fact that Coulson was sat on his lap any less awkward, with Coulson insisting that it would. With the mission now complete, Deathlok and Coulson then returned upstairs to regroup with the other S.H.I.E.L.D. agents.{{Ref|AoS512}} ====Marriage of FitzSimmons==== [[File:The Real Deal 15.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok joins [[Leo Fitz/Destroyed Earth|Fitz]] and [[Jemma Simmons|Simmons]]' wedding]] {{Quote|So, you [[Jemma Simmons|bride]]'s side or [[Leo Fitz/Destroyed Earth|groom]]'s side?"
"I know them both.|[[Deke Shaw]] and Deathlok|The Real Deal}} While the [[Gravity Containment Device]] continued to shut out all of the recreations generated from the [[Fear Dimension]], one floor from the [[Lighthouse]] had maintained a recreation of a lakeside. Deathlok was then present as [[Leo Fitz/Destroyed Earth|Leo Fitz]] and [[Jemma Simmons]]' decided to use this scene for their wedding, as Deathlok stood by the other [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] agents while Fitz and Simmons were married, as their ceremony was led by [[Phil Coulson]], making their vows to one another. As the ceremony continued, Deathlok and all of the other agents then happily clapped for Fitz and Simmons when they shared their first kiss as the husband and wife. [[File:DeathlokWatchingWedding.png|{{AltFloat}}|Deathlok asking [[Deke Shaw]] who he knows]] During the ceremony, Deathlok conversed with [[Deke Shaw]], who had awkwardly questioned if Deathlok was actually a full cyborg or simply a robot, with Deathlok quickly dismissing all of the questions, in order to continue watching the Fitz and Simmons' wedding. While Fitz and Simmons were celebrating their marriage, Deathlok then asked Shaw if he was with the bride or groom, to which Shaw replied he was with neither Fitz nor Simmons, remarking he barely knew either of them. Following the ceremony, Deathlok had departed from the Lighthouse, in order to continue his own missions away from S.H.I.E.L.D.{{Ref|AoS512}} {{Clear}} ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== {{Quote|What the hell did [[Hydra|they]] do to him?"
"What are you seeing?"
"It's not just his [[Cybertek Prosthetic Leg|leg]] or his [[Backscatter X-Ray Eye Implant|eye]] they did stuff to him, it's all under his skin.|[[Skye]] and [[Phil Coulson]]|End of the Beginning}} *'''Artificially Enhanced Physiology/Bionic Enhancement''': [[File:Deathlok x-ray.png|thumb|250px|An X-Ray view of Deathlok's Enhancements]] Due to use of the [[Centipede Device]], a blood-filtering device made of [[Chitauri]] metal that delivers a combination of [[Extremis]], [[gamma radiation]], and elements of the [[Super Soldier Serum]] into his system, Michael Peterson has come to possess various superhuman abilities. Unlike standard Centipede soldiers, he does not require regular dosage to maintain his powers; after he was shot with the [[Night-Night Gun]], his body was able to "absorb" and stabilize the serum. His powers were further enhanced by a series of bionic modifications he gained after he became a cyborg. His new upgrade also gave him a new ability to upload information into his mind and gain expertise in an ability. He was able to use it to instantly learn how to fly a Quinjet for Coulson and [[Lance Hunter]]. **'''Superhuman Strength''': [[File:SHIELD Training Facility.png|thumb|250px|Peterson pushing a large bulldozer]] Peterson's strength was increased since his earliest enhancement, the [[Centipede Device]]. He can lift much heavier objects that cannot be moved otherwise without use of machinery and overpower men and send them flying about tens of feet away. With his strength, he can tear apart any solid objects like concrete with ease. During his [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] training, Peterson was able to push a large bulldozer across a field in under a minute. In addition, his strength extends into his leg muscles as he is able to leap great distances, improved even more with a [[Cybertek Prosthetic Leg|cybernetic leg]]. **'''Superhuman Durability''': Before his transformation into a cyborg, Peterson could withstand potentially fatal physical damage, withstanding a fall from a building and the fall from the higher level of [[Union Station]] down to the ground level. Once he was outfitted with internal prosthesis, he managed to fully withstand shots from different weapons, including common and heavy firearms, a [[Taser Projectile Launcher]] and an [[I.C.E.R.]]; and even [[Lincoln Campbell]]'s electric powers. However, his head is still his most vulnerable part of his body due to it being completely exposed. **'''Superhuman Speed''': Peterson is able to move at great speeds thanks to both his physiological improvements and his [[Cybertek Prosthetic Leg|cybernetic leg]]. He managed to outrun from a full [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] tactical team, leading them to [[Thomas Nash]] under orders of the real Clairvoyant. **'''Superhuman Agility''': Deathlok's cybernetics greatly boosted his agility. He displayed this through evading a tactical team with gymnastic maneuvers, and later performing a complex flip over [[Lash]]'s [[Lash/Monolith Recreation|recreation]]. **'''Superhuman Stamina''': Deathlok is more resilient to fatigue and exhaustion, especially after getting his cybernetic implants. He showed signs of fatigue while training with S.H.I.E.L.D. before getting them, but after the upgrade, he could fight and exert himself for long periods without any sign of strain or weakness. **'''Superhuman Reflexes''': With his artificial enhancements came better reflexes allowing him to dodge incoming attacks, making him a great combatant and killer. **'''Enhanced Vision''': [[File:Melinda May (Deathlok HUD).png|thumb|250px|Deathlok's augmented visual abilities]] Thanks to the [[Backscatter X-Ray Eye Implant]] he possesses, Deathlok is capable of seeing through solid objects, which also allowed him to see in the darkness as a form of night-vision. His vision was later upgraded to include a targeting system, thermal vision, and a visual output to his audio scanner. It can also see through a [[Photostatic Veil]]. **'''Regenerative Healing Factor''': Peterson was able to heal from his injuries thanks to the [[Centipede Serum]], as evidenced when he took a shot from a shotgun but showed no significant damage upon getting up. However, he was unable to regenerate the missing leg that he lost in an explosion or the facial scars he suffered from burning, needing treatment inside an [[Hyperbaric Chamber]] to heal those wounds. ===Abilities=== {{Quote|I didn't just want to cross him off, I wanted to make a spectacle. People are killed by guns everyday in [[Bogotá]], but how often does a monster punch a [[Alejandro Castillo|drug lord]]'s head clean off?|[[John Garrett]] to [[Grant Ward]]|Ragtag}} *'''Expert Combatant''': [[File:Deathlok vs Lash TRD.png|thumb|250px|Deathlok fighting against [[Lash]]'s [[Lash/Monolith Recreation|recreation]]]] During all of his months as a [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] trainee, Peterson was taught basic combat skills to take advantage of his strength, and he managed to overpower skilled [[Centipede Soldiers|Centipede Soldier]] [[Brian Hayward]] despite getting seriously injured. As Deathlok, his fighting style changed to take advantage of his immense strength and durability, often breaking his enemies' necks or stomping them with his bionic leg. Together with Coulson, he was also able to defend himself against the duplicates of Lash and Hive. *'''Marksman''': Deathlok was able to use his arm missiles to great affect. He was able to shoot [[John Garrett]] as well as accurately shoot his missile into a [[Quinjet]] that was taking off. He also shot a Monolith Creation. *'''Acrobat''': While fighting against recreations generated by the [[Fear Dimension]], Deathlok displayed extreme agility and movement speed, easily jumping over [[Lash]]'s [[Lash/Monolith Recreation|recreation]] and fake [[Vrellnexians]] to take them down and quickly moving around them so they could not hit him. *'''Pilot''': Upon rescuing [[Phil Coulson]] and [[Lance Hunter]] from [[Robert Gonzales]]' S.H.I.E.L.D. agents, Deathlok captured and flew a [[Quinjet]] after downloading instructions and the plane's schematics. ==Equipment== ===Cybernetic Enhancements=== {{Quote|Three men, heavily armed, bet there's more where they came from."
"Does it matter? They got a cyborg on board with rockets in his arm|[[Lance Hunter]] and [[Grant Ward]]|The Frenemy of My Enemy}} *'''[[Centipede Device]]''': [[File:J-august-shield-600x331.jpg|thumb|250px|Peterson's [[Centipede Device]] on his arm]] Deathlok is outfitted with a device made of [[Chitauri]] metal which injects a [[Centipede Serum|serum]] based on [[Abraham Erskine]]'s [[Super Soldier Serum]] mixed with [[gamma radiation]] and [[Extremis]], designed to enhance his physical attributes to a super-human level. After being captured by Hydra, Deathlok was given another Centipede Device on his other forearm, as well as placed within his chestplate and embedded into his spine. However, even with the addition of two more centipede devices, his strength and other physical attributes appeared relatively unchanged. {{Quote|Good morning, Mr. Peterson. Stand by for further instructions.|[[Clairvoyant]] to Mike Peterson|The Magical Place}} *'''[[Backscatter X-Ray Eye Implant]]''': [[File:Deathlok Eye Implant.jpg|thumb|250px|Deathlok's [[Backscatter X-Ray Eye Implant]]]] A prosthetic ocular implant that allows Deathlok to see through solid objects when his eyes are closed and is also the means used by his handler to send orders and instructions. *'''Computer Enhancements''': Deathlok has two USB ports, one of which he used in his attempt to download [[Skye]]'s [[Coulson's Team Hard Drive|encrypted hard drive]]. {{Quote|That is the best money can buy, my friend. Every piece of technology in you is top-of-the-line.|[[Ian Quinn]] to Deathlok|T.R.A.C.K.S.}} *'''[[Cybertek Prosthetic Leg]]''': Deathlok's missing leg has been replaced with a cybernetic prosthetic designed and built by [[Cybertek]] that enhances his speed. It also enhances the strength of his leg, allowing him to crush a man's chest with a single stomp. It was removed and dismantled when he was captured by Hydra. *'''Ballistic Armor''': Deathlok was equipped with armor plating that can withstand high caliber gunfire, such as shotguns, with very little to no damage at all. It could also withstand an electrical shock. *'''[[Forearm Rocket Launcher]]''': [[File:Deathlok-Betrays-JohnGarrett.jpg|thumb|250px|Deathlok fires his [[Forearm Rocket Launcher]]]] Installed on to his left forearm, this unit allows Deathlok to fire guided mini-rockets at his targets. With his new upgrade from [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]], the explosives were replaced with EMP rockets, which were strong enough to take down a [[Quinjet]]. {{Quote|What did you do to [[Grant Ward|him]]?"
"Stopped his heart."
"His heart..."
"Isn't beating. He's having a heart attack.|[[Skye]] and Deathlok|Nothing Personal}} *'''[[Heart Stopper]]''': Deathlok used this on [[Grant Ward]] to make [[Skye]] unlock her hard drive, stopping his heart from beating and duplicating a heart attack. When Skye acquiesced, Deathlok tapped the device once to make Ward's heart beat, twice to accelerate the pulse. *'''Internal Prostheses''': Deathlok was implanted with cybernetic enhancements underneath his skin, enhancing his strength, cybernetic bodily functions, and endurance even more. ===Weapons=== *'''{{WPS|Kimber Solo}}''': Peterson was given the gun by [[Ian Quinn]] to test his new found loyalties to both [[Hydra]] and the [[Clairvoyant]], telling him to shoot and kill [[Skye]]; however, Peterson noted that these were not his orders and gave the gun back to Quinn. ===Other Equipment=== {{Quote|It's perfect, for an array of tactical missions."
"Yeah, also measures your heart rate, systolic pressures, and glucose levels."
"All while providing state-of-the-art ballistic protection.|[[Leo Fitz]] and [[Jemma Simmons]]|The Bridge}} *'''[[Tactical Suit]]''': [[File:Peterson Tac Suit.jpg|thumb|250px|Peterson wears the specialized Tactical Suit]] During his brief time as a [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] trainee in the field, Mike Peterson had worn a Tactical Suit designed by agents [[Leo Fitz]] and [[Jemma Simmons]]. It was made of the polymer blend with ten layers of treated composite materials, and its main function was to monitor Peterson's vitals while providing a state-of-the-art ballistic protection for him. *'''[[Hyperbaric Chamber]]''': Following his injuries, Peterson was contained inside of the Hyperbaric Chamber, as he was being transported by [[Hydra]] to become upgraded. Peterson was eventually released from the chamber by [[Ian Quinn]], once he was presented with his new [[Cybertek Prosthetic Leg|Prosthetic Leg]]. ===Vehicles=== *'''[[Skye's Van]]''': Peterson kidnapped his [[Ace Peterson|son]] and brought him to [[Skye]]'s van, forcing Skye to erase all files of Peterson's existence. Skye then drove Peterson and his son to a train station. *'''[[Bus]]''': When Peterson worked with [[Phil Coulson]]'s [[Coulson's Team|team]], they used the Bus as their base of operations. [[Jemma Simmons]] treated Peterson's wounds on the bus after a mission. Peterson was later forced to kidnap [[Skye]] and bring her to the bus, where he worked on [[Hydra]]'s orders. Peterson stood at [[John Garrett]]'s side on the Bus while Garrett took it over. *'''[[Quinjet]]''': Peterson stole a Quinjet from [[Robert Gonzales]]' faction of [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] to rescue [[Phil Coulson]] and [[Lance Hunter]]. He flew it to retrieve [[Leo Fitz]] and [[Grant Ward]], and later flew the Quinjet to [[Leo Fitz]] and [[Jemma Simmons]]' wedding. ==Facilities== *'''[[Peterson Residence]]''': Peterson lived in a house with his [[Ace Peterson|son]], although they struggled to make ends meet. Peterson talked to doctors at the [[Centipede Project]] on the phone in his house, and got frustrated, so he threw the phone at a picture of his ex-wife. *'''[[California Centipede Base]]''': Peterson was given the [[Centipede Serum]] at their [[California]] base, which later exploded. Peterson then entered the base and rescued [[Debbie Hynes]] from the explosion. *'''[[Ruthie's Skillet]]''': After saving a [[Debbie Hynes|woman]] from a fire, Peterson went to a local diner where [[Skye]] approached him and encouraged Peterson to go public. *'''[[Union Station]]''': Peterson tried to get him and his [[Ace Peterson|son]] out of town. Training Facility]]''': Peterson was brought by [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] to a training facility, where he worked to develop his powers. He was approached by [[Phil Coulson]] at the facility with a mission. *'''[[Cybertek Manufacturing Facility]]''': Peterson was forced to operate out of a [[Cybertek]] facility and confronted [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] there, until [[Skye]] communicated to Peterson that his [[Ace Peterson|son]] was safe, at which point he turned against [[John Garrett]] and crushed his skull. *'''[[Playground]]''': Peterson was taken to the Playground was that [[Leo Fitz]] and [[Jemma Simmons]] could repair his damaged cybernetic parts. He later returned to the Playground, having heard that it was destroyed by [[Aida]], and rescued the agents inside. *'''[[Lighthouse]]''': At the request of [[Deke Shaw]], Peterson went to the Lighthouse and helped Coulson through his attempt to close the rift into the [[Fear Dimension]]. [[Skye]] attempted to save Peterson, but was captured before she could. *'''[[Arctic Hydra Research Facility]]''': Peterson and [[Lincoln Campbell]] were captured by [[Hydra]] and held in their research facility for experimentation, where Peterson's weapons were deactivated.
